skill.name: iterate-pr
skill.description: Iterate on a PR until CI passes. Use when you need to fix CI failures, address review feedback, or continuously push fixes until all checks are green. Automates the feedback-fix-push-wait cycle.
skill.summary: Automates the PR iteration workflow by checking CI status, gathering review feedback, fixing issues, and pushing updates until all checks pass.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is safe for publication. Static analysis flagged 46 patterns in SKILL.md, but all are false positives. The file is markdown documentation showing bash command examples, not executable code. Commands use GitHub CLI (gh) and git with hardcoded arguments. No user input injection vectors, no external network calls beyond GitHub API, and no malicious patterns detected.

content.user_title: Automate PR Iteration Until CI Passes
content.value_statement: Pull request fixes often require multiple iterations due to CI failures and review feedback. This skill automates the entire feedback-fix-push-wait cycle so you can focus on writing code while it handles checking status, gathering feedback, and pushing fixes.
actual_capabilities: Identifies the current pull request and retrieves its status using GitHub CLI
Checks CI/CD pipeline status and waits for pending checks to complete
Gathers review feedback from human reviewers and automated bots
Fetches failure logs from GitHub Actions to diagnose issues
Validates feedback against actual code before making changes
Commits and pushes fixes, then monitors CI until all checks pass
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI (gh) to be installed and authenticated on the system
Only works when a pull request already exists for the current branch
Cannot resolve flaky tests or infrastructure issues beyond code fixes
Stops after 3 failed attempts on the same issue to avoid infinite loops

use_cases.title: CI Failure Resolution
use_cases.description: Automatically fix failing CI checks by reading logs, identifying root causes, and pushing targeted fixes until the pipeline passes.

use_cases.title: Code Review Response
use_cases.description: Process reviewer comments and inline feedback, make required changes, and push updates without manual tracking of each comment.

use_cases.title: End-to-End PR Automation
use_cases.description: Run the complete iteration loop from CI check through feedback gathering to fix deployment until the PR is merge-ready.

prompt_templates.title: Basic CI Fix
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the CI status on my current PR. If any checks failed, read the logs and fix the issues. Push the changes and wait for CI to pass.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick fix for obvious CI failures

prompt_templates.title: Review Feedback Processor
prompt_templates.prompt: Gather all review comments on my PR including inline comments. Validate each piece of feedback against the code. Address valid feedback and explain why any feedback was skipped.

prompt_templates.title: Full PR Iteration
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the complete PR iteration workflow: check CI status, wait for pending checks, gather all feedback, fix issues, push changes, and monitor until all checks pass. Stop if the same failure persists after 3 attempts.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnostic Mode
prompt_templates.prompt: Investigate why my PR CI is failing. Fetch the run logs for each failed check, analyze the errors, and provide a summary of what needs to be fixed before attempting changes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Understanding complex CI failures before fixing

output_examples.input: PR has 2 failing CI checks and 3 review comments
output_examples.output: Checked CI: 2 failed, 0 pending. Gathered 3 review comments. Fixed failing tests in test_api.py (line 45-52) and updated type hints in models.py. Pushed changes. Waiting for CI - all checks now passing.

output_examples.input: Codecov check pending, reviewer requested docstring updates
output_examples.output: Waiting for codecov check (pending). Addressed review feedback: added docstrings to UserService class methods. Skipped 1 comment - issue was already fixed in commit abc123. Pushed updates. CI passed after codecov completed.

best_practices: Always wait for pending CI checks (especially bots like codecov, linters) before gathering feedback to avoid duplicate work
Read actual failure logs instead of assuming what failed based on check names alone
Validate each piece of feedback against the code - reviewers and bots can be wrong or reference already-fixed issues
anti_patterns: Do not make changes without reading relevant code first - understand context before modifying
Do not attempt the same fix more than 3 times for identical failures - likely a flaky test or deeper issue requiring human intervention
Do not push changes while CI checks are still pending - wait for results to avoid conflicting updates

faq.question: What tools does this skill require?
faq.answer: This skill requires GitHub CLI (gh) to be installed and authenticated. It also uses git for version control operations. Both must be available in your development environment.

faq.question: What happens if no PR exists for my branch?
faq.answer: The skill will detect this condition and stop immediately, informing you that no pull request exists for the current branch. You will need to create a PR first.

faq.question: How does the skill handle flaky tests?
faq.answer: If the same CI failure persists after 3 iteration attempts, the skill stops and asks for human help. This prevents infinite loops on flaky tests or infrastructure issues.

faq.question: Does this skill modify code automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, but only after validating feedback. The skill reads relevant code, verifies issues are real and not already fixed, then makes minimal targeted changes to address actual problems.

faq.question: Can I focus only on required CI checks?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can use gh pr checks --required to focus only on required checks, ignoring optional ones. This speeds up iteration when optional checks are not blocking.

faq.question: What if my branch needs rebasing?
faq.answer: The skill will detect if your branch is out of sync with the target branch and stop immediately, informing you that a rebase is needed before proceeding with iteration.
